title: smoke-hot mediterranean lamb kebabs
description: a flavorful and refreshing mediterranean dish featuring lamb, herbs, and a smoky heat. perfect for a hangover cure or a high-protein meal.
instructions: Cut the lamb shoulder into 1-inch cubes and thread them onto skewers, leaving a little space between each cube.
In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, oregano, thyme, red pepper flakes, salt, and black pepper. Add the lamb cubes to the bowl and toss to coat.
Soak the wood chips in water for at least 30 minutes before grilling. This will help to prevent the wood from burning and creating a smoky flavor.
Preheat the grill to medium-high heat, or 400°F (200°C). Thread the lamb skewers onto the grill and cook for 8-10 minutes per side, or until they reach an internal temperature of 145°F (63°C).
While the lamb is cooking, toast the pita bread on the grill or in a toaster. Brush the pita bread with olive oil and sprinkle with salt.
Once the lamb is cooked, brush it with a little more olive oil and sprinkle with parsley. Serve the lamb skewers with toasted pita bread and a side of your favorite Mediterranean salad or toppings.
Let the lamb skewers rest for a few minutes before serving, allowing the juices to redistribute and the flavors to meld together.
To add an extra layer of smoky flavor, brush the lamb skewers with wood chips during the last few minutes of cooking.
To make this recipe more substantial, serve the lamb skewers with a side of hummus, tabbouleh, or roasted vegetables.
To make this recipe more portable, serve the lamb skewers on a platter or in a wrap with your favorite toppings.
To make this recipe more festive, serve the lamb skewers with a side of grilled halloumi cheese and a drizzle of honey.
To make this recipe more interactive, serve the lamb skewers as a DIY kebab bar with a variety of toppings and sauces.
To make this recipe more flavorful, serve the lamb skewers with a side of preserved lemon and olives.
To make this recipe more elegant, serve the lamb skewers on a platter with a side of roasted vegetables and a drizzle of balsamic glaze.
To make this recipe more comforting, serve the lamb skewers with a side of warm pita bread and a drizzle of hummus.
To make this recipe more exotic, serve the lamb skewers with a side of grilled pineapple and a sprinkle of toasted coconut flakes.
To make this recipe more convenient, serve the lamb skewers in a wrap with your favorite toppings and a side of mixed greens salad.
To make this recipe more indulgent, serve the lamb skewers with a side of crispy falafel and a drizzle of tahini sauce.
To make this recipe more satisfying, serve the lamb skewers with a side of roasted sweet potatoes and a sprinkle of chopped fresh herbs.
To make this recipe more impressive, serve the lamb skewers on a platter with a side of grilled asparagus and a drizzle of lemon butter.
To make this recipe more fun, serve the lamb skewers as a DIY kebab bar with a variety of toppings and sauces.
To make this recipe more easy-going, serve the lamb skewers with a side of warm pita bread and a drizzle of olive oil.
To make this recipe more quick, serve the lamb skewers in a wrap with your favorite toppings and a side of mixed greens salad.
To make this recipe more decadent, serve the lamb skewers with a side of crispy bacon and a drizzle of balsamic glaze.
